description: Ion OptimizeIteratorIndices used block-level dominance where instruction-level dominance was required. In js/src/jit/IonAnalysis.cpp, the pass tried to share Object.keys iterator state when another iterator's block dominated the current block. For two Object.keys operations in the same basic block, block()->dominates() returned true even when the candidate iterator instruction appeared later in the block, allowing a use-before-def rewrite after an intervening indexed load. The malformed MIR graph trips AssertExtendedGraphCoherency during Ion compilation and can otherwise miscompile iterator-index accesses. The fix uses MInstruction::dominates(ins), which checks same-block instruction order before falling back to block dominance.
